title: Widely accepted non-ANSI style task argument declarations are now treated as errors
body:
A simple example:
````
module test();

task print_hex;

  input n;

  reg [7:0] n;

  begin
    $display("%h", n);
  end  
endtask

initial begin
  print_hex(66);
end
   
endmodule
````
This code compiles without errors or warnings on all other simulators I've tried. But since PR #767, it is rejected by Icarus Verilog with the error message:
````
test.v:7: error: Scalar port ``n'' has a vectored net declaration [7:0].
````
Unlike for module ports, the IEEE standard doesn't specify what is/isn't allowed.

I found this when compiling the Micron DDR3 DRAM model, so there is real code out there that uses this form of declaration.
